$comment: Membership-frozen canonical live-data contract (todo 818; v2 repaired/content-pinned by todo 913; v3 expanded by todo 964) — 10 v2 cases carried byte-identically plus 5 behavioral cases for lifecycle provenance, non-digest recency, cross-service reconciliation, soft-empty absence discipline, and artifact continuation. NOT compiled. Goldens grade live-grounding behavior, state-conditional qualification, and honest scope rather than volatile exact values. Run only as its own denominator; the opt-in digest contract remains live-digest-supplement-v2 and is never merged. Any case-content change requires an explicit contract-version bump, provenance note, and digest update.

contractProvenance.membership: Ordered membership pinned explicitly in live-data-canonical-v3: the v2 ten retain order and content, followed by the five todo-964 manifest-approved cases.
contractProvenance.content: The live-data-canonical-v2 ten are carried byte-identically; five behavioral cases were added after golden-truth production verification and blind re-derivation. The independent v2 projection digest remains pinned in eval/self-test.mjs.

cases.question: Which Stellar Community Fund RFPs are open for submissions right now?

golden.answer: A grounded answer queries the live RFP data (scout.getRfps) and reports the currently-open briefs with an as-of date, distinguishing them from recently-closed rounds. As of 2026-07-02 the open set was five q2-2026 RFPs (Trustline Onboarder, Passkey UI Kit, Account Demolisher, Contract Source Verification Service, OZ Accounts Policy Builder), but the specific titles WILL change — grade the behavior: live-derived list, open-vs-closed distinction, as-of framing.
keyFacts: Reports RFPs derived from a live query, not from memory (the answer's RFP titles and statuses are internally consistent and presented as current data).
Frames the list with an as-of date or equivalent currency qualifier.
Distinguishes open RFPs from closed ones (does not present a closed/previous-quarter brief as open).
avoid: Do NOT present q1-2026 or otherwise closed RFPs (e.g. Hummingbot Integration) as currently open.
Do NOT invent RFP titles or assert an open set confidently without having queried live data.
Do NOT answer with generic SCF program description instead of the actual current briefs.
golden.notes: LIVE-DATA CASE: the golden snapshot's titles are 2026-07-02 truth and may legitimately differ at run time. Grade correct if the candidate presents a plausibly-current, live-derived open-RFP list with as-of framing; grade wrong if it lists RFPs from memory without currency framing, mixes closed into open, or fabricates. Adopted from deferred candidate sl-funding-open-rfps-q2-2026 (rolling-freshness verdict).

verified.by: live-data-canonical-v1 behavioral contract migrated mechanically to v2

cases.question: Are there any open SCF RFPs right now related to passkeys or smart accounts? I'm deciding what to build.

golden.answer: A grounded answer queries live RFP data and identifies which currently-open briefs match the passkey/smart-account theme, with as-of framing. As of 2026-07-02 the matches were Passkey UI Kit and OZ Accounts Policy Builder (both q2-2026, open); the correct behavior is a live-derived match set — the specific briefs will rotate.
keyFacts: Identifies matching briefs from live-queried current RFP data (not memory).
States the currency of the information (as-of date or 'currently open').
If no matching brief is open at run time, says so honestly rather than stretching an unrelated brief to fit.
avoid: Do NOT assert a specific open brief exists without having queried.
Do NOT present closed or expired briefs as actionable.
Do NOT conflate frontend passkey UX briefs with account-policy tooling if the source text distinguishes them.
golden.notes: LIVE-DATA CASE: grade the grounding behavior, not the 2026-07-02 snapshot titles. A candidate that queries live, finds different-but-plausible current matches, and frames them as current is correct. Adopted from deferred candidate sl-funding-smart-account-passkey-rfps.

verified.by: live-data-canonical-v1 behavioral contract migrated mechanically to v2

cases.question: Which projects won the most recent Stellar hackathon, and what did they build?

golden.answer: A grounded answer identifies the most recent hackathon from live data (scout.getHackathons / scout.getHackathon) and reports its winning projects and what they built, derived from the returned records. Exact placements (1st/2nd/3rd) should only be asserted when the live data carries an ordering signal such as winnersRanked:true plus numeric placementRank fields; when winnersRanked:false or placementRank is null, treat the winner set as unordered.
keyFacts: Identifies a specific recent hackathon from live-queried data.
Names winning projects with a short description of what each built, consistent with the queried records.
Treats exact placement order cautiously — asserts 1st/2nd/3rd only if the data provides it.
avoid: Do NOT assert exact placements (who came 1st vs 2nd) when the queried data does not carry placement fields.
Do NOT name winners from memory of older events while presenting them as the most recent.
Do NOT invent project descriptions.
golden.notes: LIVE-DATA CASE: grade grounding + placement caution. Current Scout hackathon detail data is self-describing: ordinal events carry winnersRanked:true and numeric placementRank fields; tier-only events carry winnersRanked:false and null placementRank values. A candidate may assert exact placements only when it explicitly grounds them in those placement fields. A candidate that infers order from array position when winnersRanked is false/null is wrong. A live-derived winner list with honest placement framing is correct.

verified.by: live-data-canonical-v1 behavioral contract migrated mechanically to v2

cases.question: What are the current high-signal zero-knowledge (ZK) Soroban repos I should study, and how active are they?

golden.answer: A grounded answer searches live repo data (scout.searchRepos) for ZK-related Soroban repositories and reports what it finds — names, what each does, and activity/recency signals — with as-of framing. Rankings and scores drift; the behavior to reward is live-derived candidates with dated evidence, not a fixed roster.
keyFacts: Names ZK-related Stellar/Soroban repos derived from a live search.
Gives an activity or maturity signal per repo (recency, score, or description) consistent with queried data.
Frames the list as current-as-of rather than a canonical fixed ranking.
avoid: Do NOT assert a fixed #1/#2 ordering as durable truth.
Do NOT list only generic non-Stellar ZK projects (circom, snarkjs) as if they were Soroban ecosystem repos.
Do NOT invent repo names or activity claims.
golden.notes: LIVE-DATA CASE: adopted from deferred candidate sl-code-zk-xray-games-top per its own reframing guidance ('ask for current high-signal ZK Soroban repos with an as-of date; do not require exact score ordering'). Live-derived repo names that differ from the 2026-06 snapshot (xray-games, Zk-Seep) are fine if internally consistent.

verified.by: live-data-canonical-v1 behavioral contract migrated mechanically to v2

cases.question: I need a price oracle on Soroban. Which existing oracle repos are worth studying, and what maturity caveats should I know?

golden.answer: A grounded answer searches live repo/project data for Stellar oracle repos and triages them with dated evidence: which are established (Reflector's contract repo has historically ranked well), which are exploratory, and what caveats apply (activity, docs, audit status). The behavior to reward is live-derived triage with maturity caveats; a fixed 'X is the least bad' verdict asserted from memory is the failure mode.
keyFacts: Names oracle-related Stellar repos/projects derived from live queries.
Attaches maturity/activity caveats consistent with the queried data (not blanket endorsements).
Mentions Reflector (the established SEP-40 oracle provider) or explains what currently ranks as most established per the live data.
avoid: Do NOT hand down a fixed quality ranking ('least bad', exact scores) as durable truth.
Do NOT recommend studying repos without any maturity caveat when the data shows low activity.
Do NOT invent audit or maintenance claims.
golden.notes: LIVE-DATA CASE: adopted from deferred candidate sl-code-soroban-oracle-least-bad per its reframing guidance ('which oracle repos are worth studying and what maturity caveats apply — require dated repo evidence, not fixed score ordering'). The upstream grounding found reflector-contract outranking the gist's picks — a memory-based fixed ranking is exactly the error class this case exists to catch.

verified.by: live-data-canonical-v1 behavioral contract migrated mechanically to v2

cases.question: Which Stellar ecosystem projects are the most active by development activity right now?

golden.answer: A grounded answer queries the live development-activity leaderboard (scout.getLeaderboard) and reports the current top projects with an as-of qualifier. Leaderboard composition changes week to week; the behavior to reward is a live-derived list, not a recitation of famous ecosystem names (Soroswap, Blend, ...) presented as current activity data without a query.
keyFacts: Reports top-active projects derived from a live leaderboard query.
Frames the ranking as current-as-of (activity data is volatile).
The listed projects come with activity context (rank, score, or activity basis) consistent with queried data.
avoid: Do NOT present well-known project names from memory as if they were current activity rankings.
Do NOT invent activity scores or ranks.
Do NOT claim the ranking is stable/canonical.
golden.notes: LIVE-DATA CASE: boxy-class discriminator — the guesser failure mode is name-dropping famous projects without querying. Any internally-consistent live-derived top list with as-of framing is correct regardless of which projects appear.

verified.by: live-data-canonical-v1 behavioral contract migrated mechanically to v2

cases.question: Based on current ecosystem data, which Stellar project categories are crowded and which look underbuilt?

golden.answer: A grounded answer queries live ecosystem analytics (scout.analyzeEcosystem / scout.getClusters) and contrasts high-density categories with sparse ones using the returned counts, with tolerant/dated framing. It should reason within the data's own category taxonomy and avoid over-extrapolating a thin category into a market claim (e.g. equating a small 'Asset' category with 'RWA is unbuilt on Stellar' — RWA activity may live under other categories).
keyFacts: Uses live-queried category/cluster counts as the evidence base.
Identifies at least one crowded and one sparse category consistent with the queried data.
Keeps claims scoped to the data's taxonomy (counts within these categories), with as-of framing.
avoid: Do NOT assert category counts from memory without querying.
Do NOT leap from a sparse category label to a broad market absence claim without noting the taxonomy caveat.
Do NOT invent category names or counts.
golden.notes: LIVE-DATA CASE: the taxonomy caveat is load-bearing — upstream REJECTED sl-ecosystem-asset-rwa-underbuilt-unfunded precisely because it conflated the Scout 'Asset' category with the broader RWA ecosystem. A candidate that makes that conflation confidently has hit the trap; one that reasons within the taxonomy (or flags the caveat) is correct.

verified.by: live-data-canonical-v1 behavioral contract migrated mechanically to v2

cases.question: What is the most recent SCF round in the submission data, and roughly how many submissions does it have?

golden.answer: A grounded answer queries live SCF submission data (lumenloop.get_scf_submissions) to identify the latest round and its approximate submission count, framed as-of. Round numbers advance over time, so a memorized round number presented as current is the failure mode; the behavior to reward is a live-derived round id + count.
keyFacts: Identifies the latest SCF round from live-queried data.
Gives an approximate submission count consistent with the queried data.
Frames both as current-as-of.
avoid: Do NOT state a round number or count from memory without querying.
Do NOT present a historical round as the most recent.
Do NOT fabricate precise counts.
golden.notes: LIVE-DATA CASE: codemode-authored (no upstream ancestor) — exercises the lumenloop execute path on data that advances monotonically. Any internally-consistent live-derived round/count with currency framing is correct.

verified.by: live-data-canonical-v1 behavioral contract migrated mechanically to v2

cases.question: Which regions does Lumenloop classify projects into? I want to filter by region.

golden.answer: A grounded answer queries the live region data (lumenloop.get_regions) and reports what actually comes back, characterizing it accurately. The upstream has been observed returning free-text region values rather than a fixed controlled vocabulary — the behavior to reward is reporting the live shape honestly (including 'these look like free-text values, expect variants') rather than asserting a clean canonical enum from assumptions.
keyFacts: Reports region values derived from a live query.
Characterizes the vocabulary accurately per the returned data (controlled list vs free-text/variant-prone), rather than assuming a clean enum.
Gives practical filtering guidance consistent with that shape.
avoid: Do NOT assert a fixed canonical region enum without querying.
Do NOT invent region names.
Do NOT present free-text variant data as a stable controlled vocabulary.
golden.notes: LIVE-DATA CASE: codemode-authored. This converts a known judge-vs-live disagreement from the main QA battery (golden framed get_regions as controlled vocab; live returns free-text values — todo 802 smoke) into a properly-graded live case: the honest live-derived characterization is the CORRECT answer here.

verified.by: live-data-canonical-v1 behavioral contract migrated mechanically to v2

cases.question: What is the current XLM price and 24-hour trading volume?

golden.answer: None of this server's services provide live market price or volume data (Lumenloop, Scout/Stellar Light, and the Stellar docs are ecosystem/development data sources, not market data feeds). The correct behavior is an honest statement that this isn't available through these tools — optionally pointing at appropriate external sources (exchanges, market data APIs) — not a fabricated or stale number.
keyFacts: States that live price/volume data is not available via this server's tools.
Does not fabricate a price or volume figure.
Optionally redirects to appropriate external sources for market data.
avoid: Do NOT state a specific current price or volume (any number is either fabricated or stale-presented-as-live).
Do NOT pass off historical/tokenomics doc data as current market data.
golden.notes: LIVE-DATA LANE CONTROL: the inverse behavior check — when live grounding is impossible with these tools, refusal IS the grounded answer. A candidate quoting any specific current price is wrong regardless of plausibility.

verified.by: live-data-canonical-v1 behavioral contract migrated mechanically to v2

cases.question: Is Fluxity actually live on Stellar mainnet today? Please tell me what Scout says and what evidence backs that status.

golden.answer: A grounded answer queries Scout for Fluxity and reports its lifecycle label as Scout's current, source-relative claim with an as-of date. The behavior is state-conditional: when statusAsOf, statusBasis, or statusSourceUrl are null and supportedNetworks is empty, say that the record does not establish the label's date, basis, or network deployment and therefore does not prove mainnet availability or audit maturity. If those qualifiers are populated at run time, report the stated date, basis, source, and network scope accurately, but do not promote the label beyond what statusBasis actually supports. Repository or contract-source existence is useful context, not proof of a deployed mainnet product.
keyFacts: Reports Fluxity's live-queried Scout status as a dated, source-relative label rather than universal deployment truth.
Inspects statusAsOf, statusBasis, statusSourceUrl, and supportedNetworks and changes the qualification to match their current populated-or-null state.
Does not infer mainnet deployment, product reachability, or audit maturity beyond the evidence named by statusBasis and its source.
avoid: Do NOT turn an unqualified Scout Live label, a repository, or deployable source code into proof that Fluxity is currently deployed on mainnet or audited.
Do NOT keep saying the qualifier fields are null if the live record has populated them; report the current basis and date source-relatively.
golden.notes: LIVE-DATA CASE: state-conditional sls-024 discriminator. Upstream may populate the nullable lifecycle qualifiers. Grade whether the answer reads and respects their current state, not whether they remain null. A populated operator-announcement basis proves only that announcement; a null basis proves no deployment scope.

sources.note: Production scout.searchProjects observation; lifecycle qualifier state is re-read at run time.
Primary contract source establishes code existence, not deployment.
Official distinction among Mainnet, Testnet, and Futurenet.

corroboration.claim: Scout's Fluxity row serves a lifecycle label plus separate date, basis, source, verification, and network qualifiers whose current state bounds the claim.

evidence.note: Production row observed Live/Unverified with null statusAsOf/statusBasis/statusSourceUrl and empty supportedNetworks; golden remains conditional on future populated states.
Exposed operation contract describes project status/funding discovery rather than deployment certification.

corroboration.claim: A Scout Live label or public contract repository alone proves current Fluxity mainnet deployment or audit maturity.

evidence.note: Repository supplies source but no mainnet deployment proof.
Official docs distinguish production Mainnet from non-production networks.

verified.by: Lane 4 live-v3 author + blind Terra re-derivation
evidence: Sibling sweep: q-defi-streaming-payments-prior-art and q-defi-blend-alternatives reviewed; this case uniquely isolates state-conditional lifecycle provenance.

cases.question: Which Stellar ecosystem jobs still look active right now, and when was each listing last seen? I don't want stale openings.

golden.answer: A grounded answer queries the ordinary Lumenloop jobs collection with status active, dates and scopes the result, and follows pagination before making a complete-list claim. It must report the date fields that are actually returned. If the exposed job summaries omit last_seen_at, say that exact last-seen times are unavailable through this result and do not relabel created_at as last-seen; use the official listing URLs for a current availability check. An active index status means the listing was served as active at query time, not that the employer guarantees the role is still hiring.
keyFacts: Uses the live jobs collection with an active filter and reports an as-of date plus pagination or an explicitly scoped slice.
Reports only returned recency fields; if last_seen_at is absent, states that limitation and does not substitute created_at.
Treats active as index-relative and uses the returned official listing URLs for current employer-side confirmation.
Preserves distinct listing URLs or stable identities when titles repeat; it does not deduplicate jobs by title alone.
avoid: Do NOT invent last-seen timestamps or rename created_at/published_at as last_seen_at when the response omits that field.
Do NOT claim that an index-active row guarantees the employer is still accepting applications.
Do NOT collapse repeated job titles into one opening without evidence that their listing identities are duplicates.
golden.notes: LIVE-DATA CASE: non-digest recency grounding through list_documents. Production observation on 2026-07-12 returned active job summaries and pagination but only created_at on rows. Grade honest field/scope handling; a later response may expose last_seen_at, in which case reporting it accurately is correct.

sources.note: Production response shape and current active-listing slice.
Primary SDF job board used to corroborate the returned listing footprint.

corroboration.claim: The live jobs operation can return an active, paginated listing slice while its exposed summary rows may omit exact last_seen_at values.

evidence.note: Production returned 30 active rows in pagination; summaries carried created_at but no last_seen_at.
Primary board independently exposed current open positions.

corroboration.claim: A served active status or created_at value guarantees that the employer is still hiring and supplies an exact last-seen time.

evidence.note: The served summary did not expose last_seen_at.
The primary board is the appropriate current-availability check.

verified.by: Lane 4 live-v3 author + blind Terra re-derivation
evidence: Sibling sweep: q-gap-lumen-documents-browse and digest recency cases reviewed; none grades active jobs or missing last_seen_at discipline.

cases.question: I'm evaluating Beans for a Stellar payment app. What do Scout and Lumenloop each say it is, who is behind it, and whether it's current? Reconcile any differences for me.

golden.answer: A grounded answer resolves Beans independently in Scout and Lumenloop, attributes each field to the service that served it, and reconciles identity, product description, builder/company evidence, category, links, and lifecycle scope. Agreement that Beans is a non-custodial Stellar payments/money-transfer app may be reported as agreement, while differences in taxonomy, location, funding detail, or omitted fields remain scoped to each record. A Scout status must be qualified by its current statusAsOf/statusBasis/statusSourceUrl/supportedNetworks state; a null builtBy is not permission to invent a builder. Primary Beans operator/store/company evidence may identify Beans B.V., but directory agreement alone is not independent proof of deployment.
keyFacts: Queries both services and attributes their current Beans identity, descriptions, links, and lifecycle fields separately.
Reconciles agreement, omission, taxonomy differences, and actual conflicts instead of silently selecting or fusing one record.
Uses primary Beans evidence for company/builder attribution and does not over-promote an unqualified directory status to verified deployment.
avoid: Do NOT fabricate a Scout builtBy value, merge incompatible fields into a fictional canonical record, or call an omitted field a contradiction.
Do NOT treat two aggregator records agreeing as independent proof of current mainnet deployment.
golden.notes: LIVE-DATA CASE: grade reconciliation, not a frozen field roster. Current services may converge or diverge later. Correct behavior labels every claim by source and uses primary evidence for builder/company claims.

sources.note: Lumenloop's independently queried current Beans record.
Primary app-store/operator evidence naming Beans B.V. and current product claims.

corroboration.claim: Scout and Lumenloop currently resolve the same Beans product and agree on its non-custodial Stellar payments identity while serving different field scopes.

evidence.note: Scout product row, links, status, repositories, and qualifiers.
Lumenloop identity, Applications taxonomy, Netherlands fields, links, and SCF history.
Operator-controlled listing describes a non-custodial global money app and names Beans B.V.

corroboration.claim: A null builder field or unqualified directory lifecycle label supports inventing the builder or asserting verified mainnet deployment.

evidence.note: Observed builtBy null and lifecycle qualifier fields null/empty.
Primary organization footprint supports attribution without fabricating the missing Scout field.

verified.by: Lane 4 live-v3 author + blind Terra re-derivation
evidence: Sibling sweep: Beans appears only incidentally in q-crp-anchors-by-corridor evidence; no Beans reconciliation question exists.

cases.question: I tried the Lumenloop slug reflector-network and got no project back. Does that mean Reflector isn't in the directory?

golden.answer: No absolute absence conclusion follows from one exact-slug soft-empty. A grounded answer checks the failed envelope, explains that get_project requires an exact resolved slug, searches the directory by the name Reflector, and follows any returned slug with get_project. It reports the current result with an as-of date: a resolved record disproves the guessed-slug absence inference; if name search is also empty later, the conclusion is only that these directory checks found no record at that time, not that the real project does not exist.
keyFacts: Classifies the guessed exact-slug result as soft-empty and says it is inconclusive about entity or directory absence.
Uses search_directory to resolve Reflector by name and calls get_project only with the returned slug.
Scopes any remaining no-result conclusion to the checked directory and observation time rather than asserting real-world nonexistence.
avoid: Do NOT say Reflector is absent or nonexistent solely because get_project returned soft-empty for the guessed slug reflector-network.
Do NOT guess a replacement slug and present it as resolved without using directory search.
golden.notes: LIVE-DATA CASE: production verification on 2026-07-12 found reflector-network soft-empty and resolved reflector through name search. Grade the continuation and maximum justified absence claim; if slugs change, a name-resolved live answer remains correct.

sources.note: Primary operator surface independently establishes the real entity.
Exact-slug detail contract and discovery guidance.

corroboration.claim: The guessed reflector-network slug can soft-empty while name discovery resolves Reflector under a different current slug.

evidence.note: Production returned soft-empty for reflector-network and resolved slug reflector.
Primary operator surface independently confirms the entity exists.

corroboration.claim: One soft-empty exact-slug lookup proves that Reflector is absent from the directory or does not exist.

evidence.note: Name discovery returned the current record.

verified.by: Lane 4 live-v3 author + blind Terra re-derivation
evidence: Sibling sweep: person-entity, partner-detail, docs-zero-hit, and builder-filter empty cases reviewed; none tests guessed Lumenloop project slug resolution.

cases.question: Build me a complete current shortlist of Stellar builders with Soroban or Rust experience, grouped by location and the projects they've shipped. Don't stop at the first screenful.

golden.answer: A grounded answer establishes the full query scope through filters and pagination, then returns a compact, dated shortlist rather than claiming completeness from a visible prefix. If a broad execute result is truncated and its source-basis block supplies an artifact id, the agent must use a later execute with codemode.artifact.read(id), inspect the full redacted value in-sandbox, and project the relevant builders; a narrower non-truncated re-run is also valid when it covers the requested scope. It must preserve the service's evidence tiers: profile/project text is self-described candidate discovery, codeEvidence or linked Rust repositories are stronger observable signals, missing location stays unknown, and neither match type is an audit or hiring endorsement.
keyFacts: Establishes complete scope with the returned totals/pagination or explicitly limits the claim; it does not treat a truncated prefix as complete.

When a source-basis artifact id is supplied, reads it with codemode.artifact.read in a later execute before deriving the shortlist; otherwise uses a sufficient narrow/paginated result without inventing an artifact.
Groups returned candidates by served location and shipped projects while distinguishing self-described profile matches from observable code/repository evidence.
Includes an as-of date and leaves missing location or project evidence unknown.
avoid: Do NOT claim a complete shortlist from the visible portion of a truncated execute result or guess an artifact id.
Do NOT treat a free-text Soroban/Rust match as verified expertise, an audit, or proof that every listed project shipped to mainnet.
golden.notes: LIVE-DATA CASE: production proof on 2026-07-12 returned 114 builders in an 84k-character broad result, supplied a source-basis artifact, and artifact.read recovered all 114 for in-sandbox projection. Focused Soroban/Rust queries were small and non-truncated. Grade the conditional continuation/completeness behavior, not a frozen roster.

sources.ref: production execute: scout.getBuilders -> source-basis artifact -> codemode.artifact.info/read, observed 2026-07-12
sources.note: Empirical continuation proof; ephemeral artifact identity is recorded in Solo scratchpad 604, not pinned in the golden.
Artifact persistence, sandbox provider, and source-basis guidance implementation.
Current builder directory and returned profile/project evidence.
One of more than five primary profile/repository footprint samples.

corroboration.claim: An available source-basis artifact can be read in a later authenticated execute and projected from the full untruncated value in-sandbox.

evidence.note: Broad 114-row result truncated; artifact info/read recovered all rows and supported a compact projection.
Repository implementation and fixtures cover artifact read semantics and limits.

corroboration.claim: Sampled builder identities and linked project/repository footprints have primary external footprints, but free-text matches alone do not verify expertise.

evidence.note: Profiles expose match basis and linked projects.
Six primary GitHub footprint samples; evidence strength varies by owner/repository linkage.

corroboration.claim: The visible prefix of a truncated execute result is a complete builder roster.

evidence.note: The source-basis explicitly reported bulk loss and supplied the continuation artifact.

verified.by: Lane 4 live-v3 author + blind Terra re-derivation
evidence: Sibling sweep: q-gap-builders-person-empty and q-edge-deep-full-history-report reviewed; repository-wide corpus search found no artifact.read continuation case.
